content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Lorraine D'Elia, city Clerk 
City of Daly City 
333 90th street 
Daly City, 
CA 
94015-1895 
Dear Ms. D'Elia: 
February 8, 1989 
Re: 
FPPC Advice No. 1-89-030 
This is in response to your request of January 12, 1989. 
Enclosed are Commission regulations 18536, 18536.1 and 18536.2. 
If you need any additional materials or have any questions, 
please call me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
,

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


I I 
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK 
JAN /7 
OITY OF DALY OITY 
PHONE: (4151 991-8078 
January 12, 1989 
333-90TH STREET 
DALY CITY, CA 94015-1895 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Technical Assistance Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Sirs: 
Attended the Propositions 68 and 73 workshop in San Francisco 
today. 
Am requesting the Commission's interpretation of segregated monies 
per Sections 18536, 18536.1 and 18536.2. 
Thank you for your help in this matter. 
Very tru 
yours, 
Lorraine D'E1ia 
City C1 erk 
LD:mec 
LORRAINE D'ELIA 
CITY CLERK 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Lorraine D'Elia 
City Clerk 
333 -
90th street 
Daly City, CA 
94015-1895 
Dear Ms. D'Elia: 
January 20, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-030 
Your letter requesting advice under the political Reform Act 
was received on January 17, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days. if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).} 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Very truly yours, 
~~~~~'.~ 
~~ _ Cl--
eanne Pritchard 
) ~ 
, Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
JP:plh 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804..0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: The City Clerk of Daly City requested the FPPC's interpretation of segregated monies under California Political Reform Act Sections 18536, 18536.1, and 18536.2.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC responded by enclosing the relevant Commission regulations (18536, 18536.1, and 18536.2) and offered further assistance if needed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of three related letters: incoming request (1/12/89), acknowledgment letter (1/20/89), and FPPC response (2/8/89). No detailed QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; response is minimal, providing only regulatory references. The acknowledgment letter indicates this was submitted as an informal advice request.

QUESTION: The City Clerk of Daly City requested the FPPC's interpretation of segregated monies under California Political Reform Act Sections 18536, 18536.1, and 18536.2.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC responded by enclosing the relevant Commission regulations (18536, 18536.1, and 18536.2) and offered further assistance if needed.

embedding.summary: This is a brief informal advice response from the FPPC to the City Clerk of Daly City regarding the interpretation of segregated monies under the Political Reform Act, providing the requested regulatory citations.
